channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: House passes immigrant detention bill that would be Trump's first law to sign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The House on Wednesday gave final approval to a bill that requires the detainment of unauthorized migrants accused of theft and violent crimes, marking the first legislation that President Donald Trump can sign as Congress, with some bipartisan support, swiftly moved in line with h
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump order halting refugee resettlement disrupting Minnesota agencies efforts
item.description: Officials with refugee resettlement agencies in Minnesota say Trump’s executive order halting the Refugee Admissions Program is disrupting efforts to bring people facing strife in their home countries to the U.S. and to the state.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: California enclave declares itself a 'non-sanctuary' city, will work with immigration officials, Trump admin
item.description: Lawmakers in Huntington Beach, California, voted Tuesday to become a "non-sanctuary" city in defiance of the state's sanctuary state law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: House Republicans seek to roll back DFL taxes, fees tied to transportation programs
item.description: Minnesota House Republicans are seeking to roll back taxes and fees approved when Democrats had full control of state government. A delivery fee and automatic increases to the gas taxes are on the GOP chopping block.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Where Donald Trump's executive orders rank historically
item.description: President Donald Trump is on pace to surpass Joe Biden's executive orders signed during his first 100 days in office
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's Day One actions reversed Biden-era health policies, including efforts to expand ObamaCare
item.description: President Trump is rolling back Biden healthcare policies, such as expansions to the Affordable Care Act – a move Democrats described as an "attack" on the federal program.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: GOP congressman wants bishop 'added to deportation list' after Trump prayer service
item.description: Rep. Mike Collins heavily criticized statements made by the Right Rev. Mariann Budde on Tuesday at the inaugural prayer service held for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Trump popularity higher than throughout most of first term: Poll
item.description: The poll found Trump with a 47 percent approval rating, higher than almost the entirety of his first term, though still below the majority support that most other presidents have enjoyed at the start of their administrations.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Working 'in tandem': Republicans prep to make Trump executive orders permanent
item.description: House Republicans are making sure a future Democratic administration cannot role back President Trump's signature executive orders.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pardons by Trump and Biden reveal distrust of each other and wobbly faith in criminal justice system
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A day that began with the outgoing president's pardon of lawmakers and his own family ended with the incoming president's pardon of supporters who violently stormed the Capitol four years ago.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump revokes Biden order allowing transgender troops in bid to rid DEI from military
item.description: President Donald Trump has revoked a Biden-era order allowing transgender people to serve in the military. There are an estimated 9,000 to 14,000 transgender troops currently serving.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Justice Department orders investigation of local compliance with Trump immigration crackdown
item.description: The Justice Department is directing its federal prosecutors to investigate for potential criminal charges against any state or local officials who stand in the way of beefed-up enforcement of immigration laws under the Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Eric Tucker And Alanna Durkin Richer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Presidents' unprecedented pardon power sparks scrutiny
item.description: The unprecedented use of pardons by former President Biden on his last day in office coupled with President Trump’s sweeping pardon of violent Jan. 6 offenders on his first has sparked renewed scrutiny of the singular power.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: House Democrats stick to work closer to home as row with Republicans persists at Minnesota Capitol
item.description: Democrats in the Minnesota House are boycotting the Capitol over a disagreement about the power Republicans should hold given a temporary advantage there. They’re doing events in their districts instead.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Trump administration puts federal diversity, equity and inclusion staff on leave
item.description: Employees in the programs will be placed on paid administrative leave. Agencies have the next few days to submit reports on how they are complying with the orders, including plans to fire them.
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie, MPR News

item.title: GOP-led states are emboldened to keep rolling back trans rights. Democrats struggle with a response
item.description: TOPEKA, Kan. (AP) — Republicans and Democrats in Kansas agree that concerns about the economy drove voters to support President Donald Trump by a 16% margin. They also know that ads from Trump and others targeting transgender rights resonated with voters.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DeMILLO, JOHN HANNA and NADIA LATHAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump defends pardons for rioters and suggests Proud Boys could have place in politics
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on his first full day in office Tuesday defended his decision to grant clemency to people convicted of assaulting police officers during the 2021 attack on the Capitol and suggested there could be a place in American politics for the Proud Boys and Oath Keepe
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pete Hegseth's former sister-in-law alleges abuse against second wife in affidavit to Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Senators vetting the nomination of Pete Hegseth for defense secretary received an affidavit Tuesday from a former sister-in-law alleging that the onetime Fox News host was abusive to his second wife, to the point where she feared for her safety. Hegseth denies the allegations.
item.dc:creator: FARNOUSH AMIRI and TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump rolls back Biden directive to study methods of lowering prescription costs
item.description: Among the dozens of Biden-era executive orders he rescinded on day one, President Trump nixed one that aimed to explore new health care payment models that would lower prescription costs for Medicare and Medicaid beneficiaries.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Minnesota and other states sue to stop Trump’s order blocking birthright citizenship
item.description: Attorneys general from 22 states have sued to block President Donald Trump’s move to end a century-old immigration policy known as birthright citizenship guaranteeing that U.S.-born children are citizens regardless of their parents’ status.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: First female Coast Guard commandant ousted
item.description: Coast Guard Commandant Adm. Linda Fagan, the first female uniformed leader of an Armed Forces branch in U.S. history, was ousted by the Trump administration with no explanation late Monday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: 5 takeaways as Trump returns to power
item.description: President Trump took office again Monday, completing a remarkable political comeback and becoming only the second person to serve nonconsecutive terms as commander in chief.
item.dc:creator: Niall Stanage, Fox 5

item.title: Trump fires 4 presidential appointees, including his former Iran envoy
item.description: President Donald announced the firing of four high-profile presidential appointees just after midnight Tuesday, including a top envoy to Iran during his first term and retired Gen. Mark Milley, who Trump tapped as Joint Chiefs chairman in 2018.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Here are the biggest fights Democrats are bracing for in Trump's second term
item.description: House Democrats are bracing for major fights in the coming year as President Trump enters the White House for his second term and Republicans control all levers of power in Washington.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump is withdrawing from the Paris Agreement (again), reversing U.S. climate policy
item.description: President Donald Trump issued a barrage of orders Monday targeting Biden-era initiatives on global warming. The decisions come on the heels of the hottest year ever recorded.
item.dc:creator: Nate Perez and Rachel Waldholz, MPR News

item.title: Trump signs executive order to pause TikTok ban, provide immunity to tech firms
item.description: The order follows TikTok going dark for about 14 hours after the Supreme Court upheld a law prohibiting the service from operating in the U.S. unless it breaks away from its parent company in China.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Trump to issue executive orders defining sexes, ending DEI practices in government
item.description: President-elect Trump intends to issue executive orders on his first day in office that will recognize only two sexes, male and female, and aim to end diversity, equity and inclusion (DEI) practices in the federal government, according to incoming White House officials.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Live updates: Trump inauguration weekend commences as protesters rally in Washington
item.description: Inauguration festivities will begin in full force Saturday as President-elect Trump and his allies — and protesters — arrive in Washington ahead of Monday's inauguration of the 47th president of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Tampa mayor meets with other U.S. mayors, responds to proposed conditions on disaster aid
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor was in Washington D.C. this weekend for the U.S. Conference of Mayors’ winter meeting. It's a time when hundreds of city leaders unite to discuss challenges and solutions involving affordable housing, infrastructure, immigration, and the climate.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers address red tape holding residents back from rebuilding after storms
item.description: The blame game continues on the local, state, and federal levels as officials discuss who can be held accountable for hurricane relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC
